title: dactylorhiza sambucina lusus incarnata
intro: Dactylorhiza sambucina lusus incarnata, commonly known as the Autumn Lady's Tresses, is a rare and unusual orchid species. It is a terrestrial orchid that belongs to the genus Dactylorhiza and is known for its unique, tress-like inflorescence. The Autumn Lady's Tresses has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, and its therapeutic actions include wound healing and anti-oxidant activities. The bioactive constituents of Dactylorhiza sambucina lusus incarnata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which may contribute to its medicinal properties.

The rhizome of Dactylorhiza sambucina lusus incarnata contains glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The aerial stems and leaves contain anthocyanins and phenolic compounds, which have been reported to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The tea of Dactylorhiza sambucina lusus incarnata is used to treat digestive issues and menstrual problems, while its tincture is known to be an anti-inflammatory agent. side_effects: Dactylorhiza sambucina lusus incarnata can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, or gastrointestinal problems.
